prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on formaldehyde (H₂C=O) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF.

Method provenance:
- Property: Frontier orbital energies and HOMO–LUMO gap
- Level of theory: DFT, tier 'standard' → B3LYP / def2-TZVP (exact four-center integrals; density fitting OFF)
- Software: PySCF
- Geometry: User-supplied .xyz at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz — single-point on that geometry, not re-optimized (result is B3LYP/def2-TZVP // input geometry)
- Solvent / environment: Gas phase (no implicit solvation)
- Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet)
- SCF convergence: Converged in 8 cycles
- Integrity: trustworthy = true, status = ok
- Warnings: None (warnings array is empty; clean run)

Key results:
- HOMO (index 7): −7.6523067 eV
- LUMO (index 8): −1.7826355 eV
- HOMO–LUMO gap (headline): 5.8696712 eV
- Total energy: −114.55325 Ha (−3117.1528 eV)
- Occupied orbitals: 8; Total orbitals: 74

Koopmans-theorem derived properties:
- Vertical IP: 7.6523067 eV
- Vertical EA: 1.7826355 eV
- Electronegativity: 4.7174711 eV
- Chemical hardness: 2.9348356 eV
- Chemical softness: 0.34073459 eV⁻¹
- Electrophilicity index: 3.7914447 eV

Note: HOMO/LUMO energies are Koopmans-theorem estimates (orbital eigenvalues), not rigorously computed excitation energies. Provenance is 'computed' — this is not an experimental value.
